note: CARDIOLOGY CONSULT. 64yo M with HTN, T2DM, hyperlipidemia presents to ED with 2 hours of substernal chest pressure radiating to left arm, associated with diaphoresis and nausea. Pain 7/10, not relieved by rest. Vitals: BP 158/94, HR 98, RR 20, SpO2 96% on RA, afebrile. Exam: anxious, diaphoretic, no JVD, lungs clear, regular rhythm without murmur, no peripheral edema. ECG: 2mm ST depression in V4-V6. Troponin I 0.42 ng/mL (ref <0.04). CXR unremarkable. Started on aspirin 325mg, sublingual nitroglycerin with partial relief, heparin drip, atorvastatin 80mg, metoprolol 25mg. Plan for cardiac catheterization. Risk: TIMI 4.
summary:
Presentation: 64yo M with HTN, T2DM, HLD presenting with 2h substernal chest pressure radiating to left arm, with diaphoresis and nausea, unrelieved by rest.
Key Findings: BP 158/94, HR 98, SpO2 96% RA. ECG with 2mm ST depression V4-V6. Troponin I 0.42 (elevated). CXR unremarkable.
Assessment: NSTEMI, TIMI risk score 4.
Plan: Aspirin 325mg, heparin drip, atorvastatin 80mg, metoprolol 25mg, sublingual nitroglycerin. Proceed to cardiac catheterization.

note: PROGRESS NOTE - CHF. 78yo F with HFrEF (EF 30%), CKD stage 3, AFib on apixaban, admitted 3 days ago for acute decompensated heart failure. Reports improved dyspnea, now able to lie flat. Down 4.2 kg since admission with IV furosemide. Vitals: BP 112/68, HR 72 irregular, SpO2 95% RA. Exam: JVP 8cm, bibasilar crackles improved, trace bilateral lower extremity edema. BNP down from 1840 to 920. Cr 1.6 (baseline 1.4). K 4.1. Transitioned to oral torsemide 40mg daily. Continue carvedilol, sacubitril/valsartan, spironolactone. Goal euvolemia, anticipate discharge in 1-2 days.
summary:
Presentation: 78yo F with HFrEF (EF 30%), CKD3, AFib on apixaban, hospital day 3 for acute decompensated heart failure; dyspnea improved, now orthopnea-free.
Key Findings: Net diuresis 4.2 kg. BP 112/68, HR 72 irregular. JVP 8cm, improving crackles, trace LE edema. BNP 1840 to 920. Cr 1.6 (baseline 1.4), K 4.1.
Assessment: Acute on chronic HFrEF, responding to decongestion; mild stable renal bump.
Plan: Transition IV furosemide to oral torsemide 40mg daily. Continue carvedilol, sacubitril/valsartan, spironolactone. Target euvolemia; discharge in 1-2 days.

note: CARDIOLOGY CONSULT - SYNCOPE. 55yo M, no significant PMH, referred after witnessed syncope while jogging. Brief LOC, spontaneous recovery, no post-ictal confusion. Denies chest pain or palpitations prior. FHx: father died suddenly at 49. Vitals normal. Exam: harsh crescendo-decrescendo systolic murmur at LSB increasing with Valsalva, brisk carotid upstroke. ECG: LVH with deep septal Q waves. Echo: asymmetric septal hypertrophy 19mm, LVOT gradient 64 mmHg at rest, SAM of mitral valve, EF 70%. Consistent with hypertrophic obstructive cardiomyopathy. Started metoprolol, counseled against competitive athletics, referred for genetic testing and ICD risk stratification.
summary:
Presentation: 55yo M with exertional syncope while jogging; brief LOC with spontaneous recovery. Family history of sudden death (father, age 49).
Key Findings: Systolic murmur at LSB augmenting with Valsalva. ECG with LVH and septal Q waves. Echo: asymmetric septal hypertrophy 19mm, resting LVOT gradient 64 mmHg, systolic anterior motion of mitral valve, EF 70%.
Assessment: Hypertrophic obstructive cardiomyopathy (HOCM) with exertional syncope.
Plan: Start metoprolol. Restrict competitive athletics. Refer for genetic testing and ICD/sudden-death risk stratification.

note: ARRHYTHMIA CLINIC. 70yo F with new-onset palpitations and fatigue x2 weeks. PMH HTN. Vitals: BP 138/82, HR 128 irregularly irregular, SpO2 97%. Exam: irregular rhythm, no murmurs, no edema, no signs of heart failure. ECG: atrial fibrillation with RVR, no acute ischemic changes. TSH normal. Echo: normal LV function EF 60%, mild LA enlargement, no valvular disease. CHA2DS2-VASc score 3. HAS-BLED 1. Rate controlled with diltiazem with HR to 84. Started apixaban 5mg BID for stroke prophylaxis. Discussed rate vs rhythm control; elected rate control with outpatient follow-up.
summary:
Presentation: 70yo F with HTN presenting with 2 weeks of palpitations and fatigue.
Key Findings: HR 128 irregularly irregular, BP 138/82. ECG: atrial fibrillation with RVR, no ischemia. Normal TSH. Echo: EF 60%, mild LA enlargement, no valvular disease. CHA2DS2-VASc 3, HAS-BLED 1.
Assessment: New-onset atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response; elevated stroke risk.
Plan: Rate control with diltiazem (HR improved to 84). Start apixaban 5mg BID. Rate-control strategy with outpatient cardiology follow-up.

note: POST-MI FOLLOW-UP. 60yo M s/p anterior STEMI with DES to LAD 6 weeks ago. Reports good functional capacity, walking 30 min daily, no angina or dyspnea. Adherent to medications. Vitals: BP 118/74, HR 62. Exam: well-healed radial access site, no murmurs, no edema, lungs clear. Repeat echo: EF improved from 40% to 48%, no wall motion abnormality at rest. Lipid panel: LDL 58 (goal <55). On aspirin, ticagrelor, atorvastatin 80mg, lisinopril, metoprolol succinate. Increased atorvastatin discussion deferred; added ezetimibe 10mg to reach LDL goal. Enrolled in cardiac rehab. Continue DAPT for 12 months.
summary:
Presentation: 60yo M, 6 weeks status post anterior STEMI with drug-eluting stent to LAD; asymptomatic with good functional capacity, medication-adherent.
Key Findings: BP 118/74, HR 62. Echo EF improved 40% to 48%. LDL 58 (goal <55). Healthy access site, no failure signs.
Assessment: Recovering LV function post-STEMI; LDL slightly above goal.
Plan: Add ezetimibe 10mg to lower LDL. Continue aspirin, ticagrelor (DAPT through 12 months), atorvastatin 80mg, lisinopril, metoprolol succinate. Enroll in cardiac rehab.

note: VALVE CLINIC. 81yo M with exertional dyspnea and one episode of presyncope. PMH HTN, COPD. Vitals: BP 124/70, HR 76. Exam: late-peaking harsh systolic ejection murmur at RUSB radiating to carotids, diminished and delayed carotid upstrokes (parvus et tardus), soft S2. Echo: severe aortic stenosis, valve area 0.8 cm2, mean gradient 48 mmHg, peak velocity 4.4 m/s, EF 55%, mild concentric LVH. Coronary CT angiography: no significant CAD. Symptomatic severe AS. Referred to structural heart team for TAVR evaluation given age and surgical risk.
summary:
Presentation: 81yo M with HTN and COPD presenting with exertional dyspnea and presyncope.
Key Findings: Late-peaking systolic ejection murmur at RUSB radiating to carotids; parvus et tardus; soft S2. Echo: severe aortic stenosis (AVA 0.8 cm2, mean gradient 48 mmHg, peak velocity 4.4 m/s), EF 55%. Coronary CTA without significant CAD.
Assessment: Symptomatic severe aortic stenosis.
Plan: Refer to structural heart team for TAVR evaluation given advanced age and surgical risk.

note: INPATIENT CONSULT. 47yo F with SLE presents with pleuritic chest pain worse when supine, improved leaning forward, x3 days, low-grade fever. Vitals: BP 110/70, HR 104, Temp 38.1C, SpO2 98%. Exam: three-component friction rub at left sternal border, no pulsus paradoxus, JVP normal, no edema. ECG: diffuse concave ST elevation with PR depression. Troponin mildly elevated at 0.09. Echo: small circumferential pericardial effusion, no tamponade physiology, EF 60%. Consistent with acute pericarditis with myocardial involvement (myopericarditis). Started high-dose ibuprofen and colchicine; activity restriction advised. Rheumatology consulted re: lupus flare.
summary:
Presentation: 47yo F with SLE presenting with 3 days of pleuritic, positional chest pain (worse supine, better leaning forward) and low-grade fever.
Key Findings: Temp 38.1C, HR 104. Three-component friction rub. ECG: diffuse concave ST elevation with PR depression. Troponin 0.09 (mildly elevated). Echo: small circumferential pericardial effusion, no tamponade, EF 60%.
Assessment: Acute myopericarditis, likely in setting of lupus flare.
Plan: High-dose ibuprofen plus colchicine; activity restriction. Rheumatology consult for SLE management.

note: PREOP CARDIAC EVALUATION. 68yo M scheduled for elective right total hip arthroplasty. PMH stable CAD (PCI 4 years ago), HTN, former smoker. Reports good exercise tolerance, climbs two flights without symptoms (>4 METs). No angina, dyspnea, or edema. Vitals: BP 130/78, HR 70. Exam: unremarkable, no murmurs, no failure signs. ECG: normal sinus rhythm, old inferior Q waves, unchanged. RCRI 1. Functional capacity adequate; no active cardiac conditions. Cleared for surgery without further cardiac testing. Continue beta-blocker and statin perioperatively; hold nothing cardiac-related.
summary:
Presentation: 68yo M with stable CAD (PCI 4 years ago), HTN, former smoker, for preoperative evaluation before elective right total hip arthroplasty.
Key Findings: Functional capacity >4 METs (two flights without symptoms). BP 130/78, HR 70. Exam without failure signs. ECG: NSR with old, unchanged inferior Q waves. RCRI 1.
Assessment: Low perioperative cardiac risk; adequate functional capacity; no active cardiac conditions.
Plan: Clear for surgery without further cardiac testing. Continue beta-blocker and statin perioperatively.
